text: The IUPAC Commission on Isotopic Abundances and Atomic Weights recently released their 2011 biennial review of the standard atomic weights of the elements. The commission has changed the standard atomic weights of five elements because of more recent analyses of higher accuracy measurements of isotopic abundances and of the ranges of isotopic compositions found in natural terrestrial samples of the elements. For bromine and magnesium the standard atomic weights have been changed to the interval notation and are given as [79.901, 79.907] and [24.304, 24.307] respectively. The standard atomic weights of germanium, indium, and mercury were changed to 72.630(8), 114.818(1), and 200.592(3), respectively. The report describes the changes and includes atomic weights in their conventional format together with the full ranges of all the atomic weight data needed for trade and commerce. An updated "Periodic Table of the Isotopes" is also introduced as an educational tool. Tables of relative atomic masses and half-lives are given for selected radionuclides.
